- Center column top: purple-dots (2 rows)
- Center column bottom: purple-dots (2 rows)
- Left column middle: purple-dots (2 rows)
- Right column middle: purple-dots (2 rows)
- Center: all gray
This forms a cross/diamond pattern with purple dots.
Bottom-right panel (current state after my click):
Row 1: gray, GREEN, gray
Row 2: white, gray, white
Row 3: gray, white, gray
The original pattern (before my click) was:
Row 1: gray, white, gray
Row 2: white, gray, white
Row 3: gray, white, gray
This is a checkerboard-like pattern with white blocks at the cross positions.
So the bottom-left panel seems to show the TARGET pattern (with purple dots indicating which blocks should be changed), and the bottom-right panel is the WORKING grid.
The target pattern shows purple dots at:
- Top center (rows 1-2 of the 3x3 grid)
- Middle left (rows 4-5)
- Middle right (rows 4-5)
- Bottom center (rows 7-8)
This is a cross pattern: center-top, center-left, center-right, center-bottom.
In the bottom-right grid, the white blocks are at exactly these positions! So the goal might be to click all white blocks to turn them green, matching the cross pattern shown in the bottom-left panel.
Currently I've clicked the center-top one (turned it green). I need to click the remaining 3 white blocks:
- Middle-left: (54-56, 24-26)

- Bottom-center: (59-61, 29-31)
